metadata.source: Meridian artifacts (MRL, Control List, CSC-01, CSP SOC 2 report)

controls.title: User Entity Workspace - IP Whitelisting
controls.descriptionText: As part of user entity onboarding, the Engineering team restricts access to the production workspace of each user entity by whitelisting their IP(s).

controls.title: Access Review - Cloud Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: The CloudOps team performs a review of the administrative access to cloud and supporting tools on a quarterly basis. Users along with their privileges are reviewed with the help of CISO within a Jira ticket. In case of any discrepancies, the CloudOps team takes necessary corrective actions. The CISO reviews the result of the review within the Jira ticket and provides sign off.

controls.title: Single sign on - Cloud and supporting tools
controls.descriptionText: Access to the cloud platform(s) and the tools supporting NXSC's functionality is restricted using single sign on (SSO) enforced by Okta.

controls.title: Privilege Access Management - Cloud and supporting tools
controls.descriptionText: Privileged access to cloud platform(s) and the tools supporting NXSC's functionality is restricted and governed through IAM Policies.

controls.title: Backup Configuration - Cloud and Database
controls.descriptionText: Full backup of applicable Cloud and Databases are scheduled every day and is retained for XYZ days. Alerts, as applicable, are defined in case of failure of backup.

controls.title: Backup Replication - Cloud and Database
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured the backup copies of applicable Cloud and Databases to be automatically replicated across other regions.

controls.title: Availability Zones (AZ) - Cloud
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured the Cloud resources of NXSC to utilize multiple geographically separate availability zones.

controls.title: Backup Restoration Tests
controls.descriptionText: CloudOps team performs backup restoration and recovery tests of NXSC databases on a quarterly basis. Results of test are recorded within a Jira ticket. Failures in restoration, if any, are fixed by taking appropriate corrective actions and recording / documenting the same within the Jira ticket. The Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provides approval within the Jira ticket once the activity has concluded.

controls.title: Disaster Recovery (DR) Drill
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, Meridian conducts disaster recovery simulation / tabletop exercise for the NXSC product and its supporting infrastructure to test its business continuity and disaster recovery measures and determine the effectiveness of mitigating controls for business disruption risks. Post the activity, DR drill report is prepared by the CloudOps team, highlighting the tests conducted, test results, and improvement areas. The Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provides approval within a Jira ticket once the activity has concluded.

controls.title: Data Purging
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled configurations to automatically purge (permanently delete) data within Cloud and Databases based on expiry of lifecycle rules.

controls.title: Data Flow Diagram
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a data flow diagram for NXSC to identify and track the flow of data between internal components and external entities, including data sources, transmission paths, and system boundaries.

controls.title: Web Application Firewall
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled WAF between NXSC and the internet to monitor, filter, and block HTTPS traffic as well as any potential attacks such as distributed denial of service (DDoS) on its web application.

controls.title: Network Address Translation
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has implemented NAT within its Cloud to manage and mask internal IPs, and to securely route outbound traffic from internal systems to external networks.

controls.title: External Traffic Restrictions
controls.descriptionText: Access to the Cloud environment hosting NXSC and supporting infrastructure is restricted to authorized personnel using Security Groups or equivalent technologies.

controls.title: Security Groups - ruleset review
controls.descriptionText: CloudOps team performs Security Groups ruleset review on an annual basis by extracting the Security Groups ruleset from the Cloud platform and reviewing them with the help of Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team to identify stale / inaccurate / missing rules. In case of any discrepancies, the CloudOps team takes necessary corrective actions. The Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team reviews the result of the review within the Jira ticket and provides sign off.

controls.title: Encryption - Cloud and Databases
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled customer managed keys (CMK) / Cloud provided default encryption to encrypt data-at-rest within applicable Cloud resourses and Databases.

controls.title: Encryption - NXSC
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured NXSC to transmit application data over the internet using Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) over TLS v1.2 / TLS v1.3.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring - Cloud and Database
controls.descriptionText: CloudTrail or equivalent technology is utilized to identify administrative events, authentication events, and system events to identify inappropriate access, security threats, and incidents within Cloud Services using alerts. Alerts are configured by the CloudOps team to be re-directed to Datadog. Alerts that cross pre-defined thresholds have been configured to be sent as messages to Slack. The CloudOps team generates a JIRA ticket for confirmed true positive cases and tracks it to closure.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring - NXSC
controls.descriptionText: Luigi is utilized to identify administrative events, authentication events, and system events to identify inappropriate access, security threats, and incidents within NXSC platform using alerts. Alerts are configured by the NXSC Engineering team to be re-directed to Datadog, which is configured to send a Slack message and raise a PagerDuty incident when thresholds are exceeded. The NXSC Engineering team analyses these PagerDuty incidents and documents the details of analysis, security violation, root cause, user / group responsible, severity of the incident / breach, and prevention and mitigation steps within PagerDuty until closure.

controls.title: Capacity management - Cloud and Database
controls.descriptionText: Karpenter is utilized to maintain NXSC application availability and optimize cluster efficiency by dynamically autoscaling Cloud Kubernetes pods and instances based on workload demands.

controls.title: Reporting to User Entities
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has developed a status webpage (https://status.XYZ.com) that offers visibility into the NXSC’s response and availability metrics across all cloud service providers and geographical instances. User entities can opt to subscribe to be notified of system incidents within this webpage.

controls.title: Support procedures for User Entities
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has prepared and maintained a support website (https://help.XYZ.com/) containing resources such as product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums to help user entities effectively utilize NXSC.

controls.title: User Entity Onboarding
controls.descriptionText: During user entity onboarding, the Professional Services team informs the Engineering and CloudOps teams to provide access to the user entity through a Jira Ticket. Administrator privileges for the user entity account are provided only to personnel explicitly identified and authorized by the user entity. Once access is provisioned, ownership and management of the account are formally handed over to the user entity prior to closing the Jira ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of customers using NXSC that were onboarded between 01-Mar-26 and 30-Jun-26.
For sample of NXSC customers onboarded during audit period, ticket/emails showing that env was set up and handed over to customer.

controls.title: User Entity Offboarding
controls.descriptionText: Upon repatriation of a user entity, the Professional Services team informs the Customer Support team, based on which a Jira ticket tagging the Engineering and CloudOps team is raised. The Cloud Resources and Databases of the repatriating user entity are deleted, and relevant details are documented within the Jira ticket. Data stored within the backup files are deleted in accordance with their respective backup retention configuration.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of customers using NXSC that were offboarded between 01-Mar-26 and 30-Jun-26.
For sample of NXSC customers offboarded during audit period, ticket/emails showing that their cloud tenancy was deleted on date of repatriation.
For sample of NXSC customers offboarded during audit period, ticket/emails showing that customer was informed about deletion of tenancy.

controls.title: Customer Support Workflow
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a customer support workflow to identify and track the reporting and resolution of problems or incidents related to NXSC.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Customer Support Workflow document showing how tickets are handled and resolved.

controls.title: Customer Support Tickets
controls.descriptionText: User entities can report issues by raising an issue from the front end of NXSC, sending an email to “XYZ@meridian.example”, or raise a ticket via “https://help.XYZ.com”. These are recorded as customer support tickets within the DevRev Ticketing Tool. Agents within the Customer Support team analyses the customer query and provides resolution by coordinating with other teams within Meridian, as required. Upon resolution, the DevRev ticket is closed.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of NXSC customer support tickets raised on DevRev between 01-Mar-26 and 30-Jun-26.
For a sample of NXSC customer support tickets raised during audit period, closed DevRev Tickets showcasing how the customer issue was resolved.

controls.title: Data Ingestion, Processing, and Export
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured Luigi to monitor NXSC platform for data ingestion, data duplication, data processing, and / or data export errors through alerts. Alerts are configured by the NXSC Engineering team to be re-directed to Datadog, which is monitored by the Customer Support team. The Customer Support team analyses these Datadog alerts and raises DevRev tickets for true positive cases and documents the details of analysis, root cause, and prevention and mitigation steps within the DevRev ticket until closure.

controls.title: Data Ingestion Reports
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured a row level reconciliation process to execute before and after data ingestion to ensure the completeness and accuracy of the ingested data. Results of reconciliation are documented within a DevRev ticket.

controls.title: New Hire Induction
controls.descriptionText:
New hires are required to sign the following documents at the time of joining to acknowledge the acceptance of terms to maintain the confidentiality and integrity of information within the organization:
1. Appointment Letter consisting of Code of Ethics and Business Conduct (COBC), and
2. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA).
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of new hires (FTE and contractors) onboarded between 01-Mar-26 and 30-Jun-26.
For sample of new joiners onboarded in India during audit period, signed appointment letter.
For sample of new joiners onboarded in India during audit period, signed NDA.
For sample of new joiners onboarded in International locations during audit period, signed appointment letter.
For sample of new joiners onboarded in International locations during audit period, signed NDA.

controls.title: Employee Handbook
controls.descriptionText: Formalized employee handbook is documented to communicate expected workforce conduct, organizational policies, and associated enforcement procedures. This document is published on U-Central and accessible by all users.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Latest version of Employee Handbook.
Screenshot from U-Central showing that the Employee Handbook is published there.

controls.title: Background and Reference Checks
controls.descriptionText:
The Human Resource team is responsible for carrying out background checks for new hires before the date of hire with the help of a third-party vendor, who conducts these checks and provides a background check report to Meridian. The following background checks are performed:
► International Criminal Searches,
► International Global Watchlist,
► International Education Verification, and
► International Employment Verification.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For sample of new joiners onboarded in India during audit period, clean beckground check report.
For sample of new joiners onboarded in International locations during audit period, clean beckground check report.

controls.title: Technical Interviews
controls.descriptionText: New hires are required to undergo technical interviews prior to appointment to help ensure that skill sets match with job requirements.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For sample of new joiners onboarded during audit period, screenshot of technical interview results from Workday.

controls.title: User Trainings - New Hires
controls.descriptionText:
New hires are required to attend the following virtual trainings on Mindtickle platform during induction containing elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches:
1. Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct, and
2. Information Security and Compliance.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshots from latest new joiners Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct training showing the contents covered.
Extraction of latest results of new joiners Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct from Mindtickle.
Screenshots from latest new joiners Information Security and Compliance training showing the contents covered.
Extraction of latest results of new joiners Information Security and Compliance from Mindtickle.

controls.title: User Trainings - Active Users
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, all active users are required to attend virtual Information Security and Compliance refresher trainings on Mindtickle platform, which includes information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of active employees (FTE and contractors) as of date.
Screenshots from latest refresher Information Security and Compliance training showing the contents covered.
Extraction of latest results of refresher Information Security and Compliance from Mindtickle.

controls.title: Performance Evaluation
controls.descriptionText: Performance evaluations are carried out on a bi-annual basis for employees who have been in the organization for more than six months. The Human Resource team informs the employees and reporting managers to fill out evaluation forms within Workday HRMS tool.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of active users, performance evaluation results within Workday

controls.title: VCSOM evaluation
controls.descriptionText: Vision, Culture, Strategy, Operating Model (VCSOM) evaluations are carried out on an annual basis for employees who have been in the organization for more than six months. The Human Resource team informs the employees and reporting managers to fill out VCSOM evaluation forms within Workday HRMS tool.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of active users, VCSOM evaluation results within Workday

controls.title: Organization Chart
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has established an organizational structure to define reporting lines, authorities, and responsibilities for NXSC to meet its objectives for providing Software as a Service (SaaS) services to its user entities.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Meridian's organizational chart

controls.title: User job roles and responsibilities
controls.descriptionText: Roles and responsibilities are defined in written job descriptions to assign responsibility for security, confidentiality, availability, processing integrity, and privacy.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of designations within the active users list, job description document showing that roles and responsibilities were defined.

controls.title: Risk Management Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a Risk Management Procedure that contains guidelines for the identification, preparation, and categorization of risks to satisfy business information, legal and regulatory requirements. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Risk Management Procedure document
Screenshot from U-Central showing that Risk Management Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Annual Risk Assessment
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance team performs risk assessment of the various functions within Meridian with the support of internal Meridian stakeholders and prepares a risk register. Risks are identified based on inherent risk value, internal and external threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and potential impact to Meridian’s objectives, assets, and operations. Risks are evaluated based on impact magnitude and expectancy criteria in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability. Mitigating controls are identified to bring the inherent risk to acceptable levels and is documented within a Jira ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Latest Risk Register

Jira ticket(s) showing the Information Security & Compliance team identified, reviewed, and documented the risks associated with its various functions in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability.

controls.title: Vendor Risk Management - Onboarding Assessments
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance Team performs vendor onboarding assessments for new vendors and third-party service providers as part of its vendor risk management process. The Information Security & Compliance team requests independent compliance and security related certifications to assess whether the vendor meets the third-party information security requirements defined by Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of vendors and third party service providers onboarded during the audit period from Streamline
For a sample of vendors and third party service providers, vendor onboarding assessment reports

controls.title: Management Review Meetings (MRM)
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, Management Review Meetings (MRM) are conducted amongst the Executive Management, Steering Committee (SSC), CISO, Legal team, Privacy team, and the  Information Security & Compliance teams to ensure security initiatives are in line with company objectives and to discuss non-conformities and findings identified during information security internal audits / risk assessments / any other assessments, incidents, and their respective corrective actions were performed.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual Management Review Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes of the meeting

controls.title: Board of Directors Independence
controls.descriptionText: Meridian operates under the direction of its Board of Directors. The Board of Directors comprises of members independent from Meridian’s daily operations.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of Board of Directors

controls.title: Board of Directors Meeting
controls.descriptionText: On a quarterly basis, matters related to Meridian’s results of operations, cybersecurity updates, projections for the coming quarter, and any major changes, are discussed between the Board of Directors and key members of management.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For quarter between Mar-26 and Jun-26, Board of Directors Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes of the meeting

controls.title: Information Security Internal Audit
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, an information security management system related internal audit is conducted to assess the operating effectiveness of controls related to information security and information technology. This audit is conducted for NXSC, User Access Management, Information Security, Network Security, Endpoint Security, Physical Security, and Human Resource Security. Observations or issues identified during the audit are documented in the internal audit report along with recommendations for resolutions of the observations or issues and a management response is sought for action plans.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Internal Audit Report for the audit period
Email communication showing that internal audit report is shared with the respective stakeholders.

controls.title: Subservice Organization Assessments
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Information Security & Compliance team assesses the subservice organization’s compliance with the applicable security, confidentiality, availability, and processing integrity commitments; and evaluates the impact of any noted exceptions of relevant controls and Complementary User Entity Controls by obtaining and evaluating their annual System and Organization Control attestation report.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Subservice Organization Assessment report for AWS
Latest AWS SOC Report covering the audit period.

controls.title: Vendor Risk Management - Annual Re-assessments
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Information Security & Compliance Team performs a re-assessment for all vendors and third-party service providers as part of its vendor risk management process. This review includes obtaining and evaluating information related to their management, privacy requirements, quality system, safety and health environment, information security, business continuity management and human resources. Observations identified during the assessment are communicated to the third-party service providers and are tracked to closure by the Information Security & Compliance team.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of all active vendors and third party service providers as on date from Streamline
For a sample of active vendors and third party service providers not onboarded during the audit period, vendor annual re-assessment reports

controls.title: ISO 27001
controls.descriptionText:
2022 and PCI DSS v4.0:
On an annual basis, assessment under standard ISO/IEC 27001:2022 and PCI DSS v4.0 are performed by an external certification body.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Latest ISO 27001:2022 certificate for all locations
Latest PCI DSS v4.0 certificate

controls.title: Information Security Awareness
controls.descriptionText: On a monthly basis, the Information Security and Compliance team sends mass emails to all users on information security awareness updates and leading practices in order to spread awareness.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For all months between Mar-26 and Jun-26, email communication showing all users were made aware about information security awareness updates and leading practices.

controls.title: MSA - User Entities
controls.descriptionText: Meridian defines its terms of service; security obligations; intellectual property; availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity commitments; and compliance with laws within its Master Software and Services Agreements (MSAs) that are signed with user entities during onboarding.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of User Entities using NXSC, Master Software and Services Agreement

controls.title: MSA / SOW - Vendors
controls.descriptionText: Service levels, security, confidentiality, availability, and processing integrity commitments with all third-party service providers and vendors are agreed as a part of MSA / SOW signed with them.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of active vendors and third party service providers, Master Software and Services Agreement

controls.title: Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a SDLC Procedure that contains guidelines on best practices for training and implementation of software security during application development to maximise their inherent security. It is published on Quanta Capture and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Software Development Lifecycle Procedure maintained for NXSC
Screenshot from U-Central showing Software Development Lifecycle Procedure was published on U-Central
AI Development Lifecycle (AI-DLC) Procedure maintained for NXSC
Screenshot from U-Central showing AI-DLC Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Global Information Security Policy
controls.descriptionText:
Meridian has defined and developed the Global Information Security Policy addressing matters related to system security, availability, confidentiality, processing integrity, and privacy principles.

Additionally, the following are also defined:
► Meridian's objectives pertaining to integrity and ethical values
 i. Code of Business Conduct
 ii. Role Competencies
 iii. Disciplinary Policies
► Human Resources security policy for hiring, termination, and periodic performance appraisals of users,
► Clear desk and clear screen policy,
► Media Handling Guidelines for electronic and paper based media disposal,
► Access Control Policies for user access management, privilege management, and password management, and
► Physical Security Policy for physical security and environmental safeguards.

It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Global Information Security Policy
Screenshot from U-Central showing Global Information Security Policy was published on U-Central

controls.title: Dedicated CISO
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has a designated CISO with documented information security roles and responsibilities to oversee the development, implementation, monitoring, and communication of security policies and procedures.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Meridian's organizational chart showing CISO
Job description document showing the roles and responsibilities of CISO were defined.

controls.title: Asset Management Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented an Asset Management Procedure which contains baseline requirements for Meridian's information systems and assets. The procedure also contains guidance regarding the ownership, acceptable use, classification, monitoring, disposal, and destruction of assets. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Asset Management Procedure document
Screenshot from U-Central showing Asset Management Procedure  was published on U-Central

controls.title: Data Classification and Handling Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a Data Classification and Handling Procedure that contains guidelines to restrict and protect Meridian and its customers' information during storage, processing, and transmission. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Classification and Handling Procedure document
Screenshot from U-Central showing Data Classification and Handling Procedure  was published on U-Central

controls.title: Data Retention Policy
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a Data Retention Policy that contains guidelines for storage, retention, and disposal of data. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Retention Policy
Screenshot from U-Central showing  Data Retention Policy  was published on U-Central

controls.title: Data Backup and Restore Policy
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined a Data Backup and Restore Policy describing the guidelines for performing and restoring backups. This procedure is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Backup and Restore Policy
Screenshot from U-Central showing  Data Backup and Restore Policy  was published on U-Central

controls.title: Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined a Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Procedure describing the guidelines for plan activation, recovery strategy, impact analysis, simulation handling, rollback, and restoration procedures. This procedure is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Procedure
Screenshot from U-Central showing Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Cyber Insurance
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has procured a cyber insurance to offset the financial losses that could occur due to critical security incidents.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Latest Cyber Security Insurance policy

controls.title: Incident Response Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented an Incident Response Procedure for reporting, classification, containment, and resolution of security incidents. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Incident Response Procedure
Screenshot from U-Central showing Incident Response Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Endpoint Security and Device Hardening Standard
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented an Endpoint Security and Device Hardening Standard that contains hardening requirements to secure Meridian's endpoints, network, underlying infrastructure, and other information systems. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Endpoint Security and Device Hardening Standard
Screenshot from U-Central showing Endpoint Security and Device Hardening Standard was published on U-Central

controls.title: Whistleblower Policy
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a Whistleblower Policy that contains guidelines for employees to report concerns of unethical behaviour, allegations of fraud, or violation of the company's code of business conduct policy, regulatory rules, and laws. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Whistleblower Policy
Screenshot from U-Central showing Whistleblower Policy was published on U-Central

controls.title: Capacity and System Monitoring Policy
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined a Capacity and System Monitoring Policy to manage availability and application issues within their cloud environments. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Capacity and System Monitoring Policy
Screenshot from U-Central showing Capacity and System Monitoring Policy was published on U-Central

controls.title: Tenant Deletion Policy
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has established guidelines to govern data deletion within the cloud infrastructure and backups of off boarding user entities within the Tenant Deletion Policy. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Tenant Deletion Policy
Screenshot from U-Central showing Tenant Deletion Policy was published on U-Central

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring Policy
controls.descriptionText: Meridian maintains an Audit Logging and Monitoring Process document to manage the audit log sources, activities performed by the SecOps team, and the corrective and preventive actions taken by the respective teams. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Logging and Monitoring Policy
Screenshot from U-Central showing Logging and Monitoring Policy was published on U-Central

controls.title: Vulnerability and Patch Management Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a Vulnerability and Patch Management Procedure that contains guidelines to prevent, detect, and remediate vulnerabilities within Meridian's applications and underlying infrastructure. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Vulnerability and Patch Management Procedure
Screenshot from U-Central showing Vulnerability and Patch Management Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Third Party Risk Management Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a Third Party Risk Management Procedure that contains guidelines to describe the due diligence and oversight processes for third-party services and software application procurement. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Third Party Risk Management Procedure
Screenshot from U-Central showing Third Party Risk Management Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Phishing Simulation
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, Meridian conducts phishing simulation exercises.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Email communication and report showing that Meridian conducts phishing simulation exercises on an annual basis

controls.title: Asset Inventory
controls.descriptionText: Corporate IT team maintains a list of workstations, servers, and network devices identifying the asset ID, asset owner, asset location, asset type and model of the asset within Freshdesk.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Asset Inventory containing a list of workstations, servers and network devices identifying the asset ID, asset owner, asset location, asset type and model of the asset from Freshdesk

controls.title: Access Creation - IT accounts
controls.descriptionText: When a user joins the organization, HR team creates a user ID within Workday HRMS tool, which automatically sends an email communication to the IT team and Workspace Experience team and creates an account within Microsoft Entra.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing configuration within Workday HRMS and Azure Active Directory that an account within the local Azure Active Directory is automatically created when a user ID is created within Workday HRMS tool.
Screenshot showing configuration within Workday that the global onboarded email is sent automatically to the IT and Workplace team on the date of joining of the employee.
For a sample of new joiners onboarded during the audit period, automated Workday emails containing employee's date of joining was shared with the IT team requesting to create the AD accounts.
For a sample of new joiners,email communication showing that IT team confirmed that the access to Azure AD account is created.
For a sample of new joiners, screenshot from Azure Active Directory showing the access creation date.

controls.title: Access Revocation - IT accounts
controls.descriptionText: On the last working day of a user, whether resignation, termination, or absconding, HR team deactivates the user ID within Workday HRMS tool, which automatically deactivates the user's account within Microsoft Entra. The IT team validates the access revocation of the user on the last working day and confirms the same via email.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing configuration within Workday HRMS and Azure Active Directory that an account within the local Azure Active Directory is automatically deactivated when a user ID is deactivated within Workday HRMS tool.
Screenshot showing configuration within Workday that the global offboarding email is sent automatically to the IT team on the last working date of the employee.
For a sample of leavers, automated Workday emails containing employee's date of joining was shared with the IT team requesting to deactivate the AD accounts.
For a sample of leavers, email communication showing that IT team confirmed that the access to Azure AD account is deactivated.
For a sample of new joiners, screenshot from Azure Active Directory showing the access deactivation date.

controls.title: Access Review - IT Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance team performs a review of the administrative access to firewalls and Okta on a quarterly basis. Users along with their privileges are reviewed with the help of CISO within a Jira ticket. In case of any discrepancies, the respective team takes necessary corrective actions. The CISO reviews the result of the review within the Jira ticket and provides sign off.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For the quarter between Mar-26 and Jun-26, JIRA ticket showing firewall privileged access review for all in-scope firewalls.
For the quarter between Mar-26 and Jun-26, JIRA ticket showing Okta privileged access review.

controls.title: Password and Account Lockout Policy – Microsoft Entra
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has implemented Microsoft Entra to enforce password and account lockout policy settings on end user workstations. These policy settings include password length as 12 characters, password complexity, password history as 24 passwords remembered, and password expiry as 90 days. Multi-factor authentication is enforced for all users added to the Azure Active Directory. User accounts are locked after 5 invalid logon attempts and account lockout is reset after 30 minutes through group policy settings.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot of Password policy settings configured on Meridian Azure Active Directory

controls.title: Idle Session Timeout - Microsoft Entra
controls.descriptionText: Idle session timeout settings are controlled through group policy settings on Microsoft Entra and is configured to terminate unattended end user workstations within 10 [India] / 30 [US] minutes of inactivity with password protected screensaver.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot of group policy settings configured on Meridian Azure Active Directory showing that unattended end user workstations are terminated after minutes of inactivity with password protected screensaver.

controls.title: Hardening of network devices, servers and workstations
controls.descriptionText: MacBook and Windows laptops in all locations are provided by Meridian with Automox (Windows) / Kandji (MacBooks) for patch management, BitLocker (Windows) / FileVault (MacBooks) for encryption, Admin By Request for EPM, FortiClient for ZTNA, CrowdSrike for EDR, and Microsoft Entra for security related group policies. Laptops are configured with software and applications depending on Meridian business requirements.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of endpoints, screenshot showing Automox was installed and that the computer was up-to-date.
For a sample of endpoints, screenshot showing Kandji was installed and that the computer was up-to-date.
For a sample of endpoints, screenshot showing CrowdStrike EDR was installed.
For a sample of endpoints, screenshot showing BitLocker / FileVault HDD encryption was enabled.
For a sample of endpoints, screenshot showing Admin By Request was installed and joined to Microsoft Entra ID.
For a sample of endpoints, screenshot showing FortiClient ZTNA was installed and connected to FortiClient Cloud.
For a sample of endpoints, screenshot showing that it was joined to the Meridian Microsoft Entra Domain.

controls.title: Email Security
controls.descriptionText: Meridian uses M365 default email security configurations such as anti-spam filter to restrict malicious incoming emails and compensating Data Loss Prevention (DLP) to prevent sharing of information over email.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot of configuration settings within M365 showing anti-spam filter is configured to restrict malicious incoming emails.
Screenshot of configuration settings within M365 showing compensating Data Loss Prevention (DLP) is configured to prevent sharing of information over email.

controls.title: Data Destruction
controls.descriptionText: Documentation, workstations, and media devices are sanitized prior to disposal or decommissioning in accordance with the Asset Management Procedure.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Certificate of Data Destruction

controls.title: Firewall Rules
controls.descriptionText:
Meridian has implemented perimeter firewalls on the Bengaluru, Chennai, London, Palo Alto, New York, and Valencia delivery center internet gateways to proactively identify and protect Meridian network against security, availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity threats from sources outside the system boundaries. Rules are enforced on the firewalls to:
► ‘deny all’ traffic by default with access enabled for specific services as per business requirements,
► protect against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and IP spoofing attacks, and
► configure Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) and web filters to monitor and prevent intrusion attempts.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Management approved network diagrams for Bangalore location.
Screenshot showing 'deny all' rule is configured within firewall for Bangalore location.
Screenshot showing protect against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and IP spoofing attacks are configured within firewall for Bangalore location.
Screenshot showing  Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) and web filters is configured within firewall for Bangalore location.

controls.title: Change Management - Meridian Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: Changes to the Meridian on-premises infrastructure are logged within Freshdesk tickets and tracked to closure by the Corp IT team.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of changes to the Meridian on-premises infrastructure from Freshdesk between Mar-26 and Jun-26
For a sample change tickets within the audit period, ticket to showing description of change and actions taken to implement the change were documented within the ticket.

controls.title: Patch Management - Meridian Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText:
Meridian has implemented the following patch management tools to ensure up-to-date patches are deployed on the endpoints in accordance with the Vulnerability and Patch Management Procedure:
► Automox for Windows and Linux
► Kandji for MacBooks
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of endpoints, screenshot showing Automox was installed and that the computer was up-to-date. (Same as IT-07)
For a sample of endpoints, screenshot showing Kandji was installed and that the computer was up-to-date. (Same as IT-07)

controls.title: Segregation of Environments
controls.descriptionText: The development, staging, and production environments are hosted on logically segregated Virtual Private Clouds (VPC) within Cloud.

controls.title: Technical Specification and Design
controls.descriptionText: Authorized feature development / patch release / hotfix requirements are communicated to the NXSC developers by the Project Manager using work items within a Jira ticket.

controls.title: Coding - Peer Review
controls.descriptionText: A developer performs coding in the development environment. A peer or Technical Lead carries out a code review to ensure completeness and accuracy of the code and results of the review are documented within GitHub. Upon successful completion of the code review, the peer reviewer provides sign off within GitHub, and the developer initiates security testing.

controls.title: Security Testing
controls.descriptionText: Upon successful completion of the peer code review, a scheduled job is automatically run to perform Snyk static code analysis. Deployment of code onto production is restricted until all security checks have passed.

controls.title: Require Pull Request
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled configurations to require all commits to be made to a non-target branch and submitted via a pull request before they can be merged with the master branch.

controls.title: Deployment of code to production
controls.descriptionText: Upon successful completion of testing, the commit is merged with the master branch and closed using a pull request. As part of the CI-CD pipeline, a GitHub action automatically performs integration checks and deploys the code onto the production environment.

controls.title: Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, third-party vendors conduct application level, network level, and API vulnerability assessments and penetration tests for NXSC. Based on the findings of these tests, Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team follows up with the relevant stakeholders on appropriate corrective and preventive actions for identified gaps until closure of the gaps. Based on the confirmation received from the relevant stakeholders, Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team notifies the third-party vendor to perform rescan to confirm the closure of gaps through email. On completion of rescan, third-party vendor shares the summary report (Compliant Report) with the Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual Vulnerability assessment and penetration test (VAPT) reports for NXSC.
For open vulnerabilities, Jira ticket showing that corrective and preventive actions are being tracked to closure.

controls.title: ASV Scans
controls.descriptionText: On a quarterly basis, security vulnerability scans are performed by third-party approved scanning vendors (ASVs). Based on the findings of the vulnerability assessment, Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team follows up with the relevant stakeholders on appropriate corrective and preventive actions for identified gaps until closure of the gaps.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For quarter between Mar-26 and Jun-26, security vulnerability scan reports issued by third-party approved scanning vendor (ASV) for NXSC

controls.title: Anti-Virus
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has deployed CrowdStrike Falcon EDR software on its endpoints for protection against malware and intrusion attempts. The EDR software is configured to perform real-time scans. The EDR software has built in controls for tamper protection.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Crwodstrike EDR software showing that all endpoints are added as hosts.
Screenshot from Crwodstrike EDR software showing that agent tamper protection was enabled.
Screenshot from Crwodstrike showing that EDR software is configured to perform real-time scans to protect against malware and intrusion attempts

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring - IT Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: Administrative events, authentication events, and system events generated by CrowdStrike, Microsoft Entra, and firewalls to identify inappropriate access, security threats, and incidents are configured by the Security Operations team to be re-directed to ProACT tool. The SISA ProACTMXDR team analyses the alerts and generate a Freshdesk ticket for confirmed true positive cases. The Security Operations team analyses these Freshdesk tickets and documents the details of analysis, security violation, root cause, user / group responsible, severity of the incident / breach, and prevention and mitigation steps within the Freshdesk ticket until closure.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from ProACT showing alerts generated by CrowdStrike EDR is configured to be re-directed to ProACT
Screenshot from ProACT showing alerts generated by Admin By Request EPM is configured to be re-directed to ProACT
Screenshot from ProACT showing alerts generated by FortiClient ZTNA is configured to be re-directed to ProACT
Screenshot from ProACT showing alerts generated by Microsoft Entra is configured to be re-directed to ProACT
System generated list of SecOps Freshdesk tickets from Freshdesk between Mar-26 and Jun-26
For a sample of Logging and Monitoring Freshdesk tickets, ticket showing that Security Operations team analyzed and took necessary corrective actions prior to closing the ticket.

controls.title: Information Security Incident Management
controls.descriptionText: Users report the actual / suspected information security incident / breaches to the Security Operations team by raising a ticket in Freshdesk tool. The Security Operations team, Meridian Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT), and Legal team performs analysis of the incident / breach and documents the details of analysis, security violation, root cause, user / group responsible, severity of the incident / breach, and prevention and mitigation steps within the Freshdesk ticket until closure.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of Information Security Freshdesk tickets, ticket showing that Security Operations team analyzed and took necessary corrective actions prior to closing the ticket.

controls.title: Visitor Management
controls.descriptionText: Security guards are responsible for ensuring that details of the visitors or vendors, such as name of the visitor or vendor, contact details, contact person in Meridian, entry time and exit time are recorded within the Visitor Register maintained at the reception area prior to permitting entry to the delivery center. Visitors and vendors are issued a visitor identification card, which they are required to display while inside the delivery center. An authorized employee escorts visitors or vendors during their visit to the delivery center.

controls.title: Physical Access Reconciliation
controls.descriptionText: Physical access to Meridian Delivery Centers is reconciled on a quarterly basis by the Workspace Experience team. The Workspace Experience team extracts the list of active users that have access to Meridian delivery centers from the access control software of that delivery center and compares this list with the list of active users shared by HR team. In case of any discrepancies, the Workspace Experience team takes necessary corrective actions and shares the results of the review with the Information Security & Compliance team.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For quarter between Mar-26 and Jun-26, email communication showing reconciliation of the personnel with access to the Meridian Delivery Centers (Bangalore location) and the list of active users is performed.

For quarter between Mar-26 and Jun-26, email communication showing reconciliation of the personnel with access to the Meridian Delivery Centers (Chennai location) and the list of active users is performed.

For quarter between Mar-26 and Jun-26, email communication showing reconciliation of the personnel with access to the Meridian Delivery Centers (Valencia location) and the list of active users is performed.

controls.title: Access Review - Physical Security Tools
controls.descriptionText: Administrative access to Access Control Software is reviewed by the Workspace Experience team on a six-monthly basis. Personnel from the Workspace Experience team extracts the list of administrators on Access Control Software and shares it with Manager – Workspace Experience. The Manager – Workspace Experience reviews the list of administrators to determine the appropriateness of access based on the job responsibilities and communicates discrepancies, if any, to the Workspace Experience team. In case of any discrepancies, the Workspace Experience team takes necessary corrective actions and shares confirmation to the Manager – Workspace Experience. The Manager – Workspace Experience provides sign off over email once the activity has concluded.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of six months between Mar-26 and Jun-26, complete access review tickets/emails of the access control system for Bangalore location

It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.

controls.title: Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) and Data Inventory - NXSC

The RoPA and Data Inventories are reviewed annually by the Meridian Privacy team.

Data Inventory for NXSC

The RoPA and Data Inventories are reviewed annually by the Meridian Privacy team.

controls.title: DPA - Vendors
controls.descriptionText: Vendors are required to sign Data Processing Agreements (DPA), committing to the acceptable use of private and / or confidential data when they have access to user entity data. Agreements are updated when there are system changes that require changes to the customers’ requirements.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of vendors having access to user entity data, Data Processing Agreements (DPA)

controls.title: Consent Management - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: Formalized policies and procedures exist for obtaining, documenting and retaining consent. These policies and procedures are approved by Senior Management of Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Consent Management Policy

controls.title: Consent Tracking - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: Explicit consent is obtained from data subjects on Meridian website, Workday, and data centers prior to collection, use or disclosure of their personal information, and details such as identity of data subject, date & time of consent and method of obtaining consent are recorded and maintained within Onetrust tool. Data subjects (candidates, employees, contractors, and visitors) provide their explicit consent by signing the ‘Privacy Notice’ prior to collection, use, and / or disclosure of their personal information. Prior notices and consent records are retained as per Meridian standard retention policies.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of consents obtained from data subjects.

controls.title: Data Subject Rights Management Policy and Procedure - NXSC
controls.descriptionText: Formalized policies and procedures exist for managing the user entity access rights request related to access, erasure, rectification, restrict processing, object and right to be informed. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Subject Rights Management Procedure for NXSC
Screenshot from U-Central showing Data Subject Rights Management Procedure for NXSC

controls.title: Data Subject Rights Management - NXSC
controls.descriptionText: Meridian provides user entities with the ability to extract, correct, amend, append, and delete all recorded personal information stored within NXSC. User entities can utilize the features available within NXSC to resolve such data subject requests, or they can reach out to Meridian by sending an email to “privacy@meridian.example”. As a data processor, Meridian forwards all data subject requests to the user entity to verify their authenticity and obtain confirmation to proceed. Meridian has implemented the “product incident management” procedure for receiving, addressing, resolving, and communicating the resolution of these requests from user entities.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Subject Rights Requests raised for NXSC between Mar-26 and Jun-26
For a sample of NXSC data subject requests, email/ticket chain showing resolution

controls.title: Data Subject Rights Management Policy and Procedure - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: Formalized policies and procedures exist for managing the user / employee / applicant access rights request related to access, erasure, rectification, restrict processing, object and right to be informed. These policies and procedures have been approved by Senior Management of Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Subject Rights Management Procedure for Meridian
Screenshot from U-Central showing Data Subject Rights Management Procedure for Meridian

controls.title: Data Subject Rights Management - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: User / employee / applicant can reach out to Meridian by sending an email to “privacy@meridian.example” to resolve data subject requests to extract, correct, amend, append, and / or delete their data. The Meridian Privacy team validates the request and address, resolve, and communicate the resolution of authorized requests.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Subject Rights Requests raised for data within Meridian between Mar-26 and Jun-26
For a sample of Meridian data subject requests, email/ticket chain showing resolution

controls.title: Breach Management
controls.descriptionText: Meridian creates and retains a complete, accurate, and timely record of detected or reported unauthorized disclosures (including breaches) of Meridian data and user entity data to meet the entity’s objectives related to privacy. Breaches are acted on in accordance with established incident response procedures. Meridian provides notification of data breaches and incidents to the affected user entities, regulators, and others, as applicable.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of breaches that occurred between Mar-26 and Jun-26
For a sample of breaches, JIRA ticket/email communication showing resolution of the breach.
For a sample of breaches, email communication showing Meridian provides notification of that data breach to the affected user entities, regulators, and others, as applicable.

controls.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) - NXSC
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Meridian Privacy Team performs Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) for NXSC to evaluate and mitigate privacy risks related to the nature, scope, context, purpose, necessity, and proportionality of personal information; data life cycle management; data subject rights; and sub-processors.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual DPIA for NXSC

controls.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Meridian Privacy Team performs Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) for the Meridian Website and Workday to evaluate and mitigate privacy risks related to the nature, scope, context, purpose, necessity, and proportionality of personal information; data life cycle management; data subject rights; and sub-processors.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual DPIA report for Meridian Website.
Annual DPIA report for Workday.

controls.title: Cookie Notice - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText:
Formalized Cookie Notice for managing information collected through cookies is developed and published on Meridian Website and Workday covering the type of cookies collected, purpose, lawful ground of processing, and changes to Cookie Notice. It is approved by the Senior Management of Meridian. These notices communicate the following to the Data Subjects:
• Purpose for collecting personal information and types
• Choice, consent, and security for privacy
• Methods of collection, use, access, retention, and disposal
• Disclosure to third parties
• Quality, including data subjects’ responsibilities for quality
• Monitoring and enforcement
• Consequences of denying or withdrawing consent and / or data transfers
evidenceRequirementTexts: Meridian Cookie Notice available for Meridian's website.
Cookie Notice available on Workday.
